description_long: This object is a solar energy infrastructure installation featuring a rectangular array of photovoltaic panels mounted on a robust metallic frame. The panels are arranged in a single, slightly elevated row, with a visible support structure beneath that appears to be made of metal, likely aluminum or steel. The surface of the panels is dark, likely glass or coated silicon, and the frame includes some visible mechanical and possibly electrical components at the base. The installation is compact and low-profile, suitable for placement on flat urban surfaces such as rooftops, parking lots, or dedicated ground areas. The overall design is utilitarian, with exposed structural elements and a focus on maximizing sunlight capture. No visible cabling or inverter housing is apparent from the exterior views.
description: A compact, rectangular solar panel array mounted on a sturdy metal frame, designed for urban energy generation. The installation features a single row of dark photovoltaic panels with exposed support structure beneath.
description_view_0: Front view showing the narrow edge of the solar panel array and the supporting frame beneath.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the full length of the panel row and the underlying metallic support structure.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, with the opposite narrow edge and some visible base components.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the length of the array and the support frame details.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 20 degrees, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted, supported by a metallic frame likely made of aluminum or steel
attribute_array_configuration: single linear row of approximately 6 panels
